narrative: FY2024-25 shows a sharper split between operational workforce inclusion, governance diversity and welfare intensity. Tata Steel leads female employee share at 8.90%, with JSW second at 6.71%, SAIL third at 6.28% and Jindal fourth at 4.41%. Jindal Stainless is the clear Board-diversity leader at 27.28%, followed by JSW at 25.00%; Tata falls to 10.00% after its women directors reduce from 2 to 1, while SAIL reports 0 female directors. SAIL leads female wage share at 6.19%, narrowly ahead of Tata at 6.00%, and strongly leads wellbeing expenditure at 1.44% of revenue. Tata’s main strengths are employee-level diversity and improving wellbeing spend, but its governance-diversity signal weakened materially. Across the theme, Tata is the most balanced private-sector performer, Jindal leads Board representation, SAIL leads welfare-spend intensity and wage share, and JSW sits mid-pack except for Board diversity. The largest red flag across all peers is 0% female KMP representation.
